item.title: A Sprawling Middle East War Explodes
item.description: A joint U.S.-Israeli attack on Iran, followed by Iranian retaliation, has engulfed much of the Middle East in conflict. Crisis Group experts offer a 360-degree view of the new war’s initial reverberations in the region.

item.title: Operation Epic Folly? Trump’s and Netanyahu’s War on Iran
item.description: Crisis Group experts discuss the U.S.-Israel war with Iran, its fallout in the Gulf and wider region, and what might come next. The analysis focuses on the strategic implications and potential evolutions of the conflict.

item.title: Cold Hard Truths: Arctic Security in a Changing World
item.description: Arctic security, Donald Trump’s ambitions to acquire Greenland, and Russia’s and China’s interests in the High North are discussed. The response of NATO and European capitals to these geopolitical developments is analyzed.

item.title: U.S. Snaps up Venezuela’s Oil and Rare Minerals in Race for Supplies
item.description: The Trump administration is eyeing Venezuela’s natural resources, with increased urgency to access petroleum and critical minerals. Crisis Group experts explain the challenges ahead in this race for supplies.

item.title: Oil Supply Risks Mount as Iran Lays Mines in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iran has begun laying naval mines in the strategically vital Strait of Hormuz, a move that could extend the effective blockade of the world’s most critical energy chokepoint. This activity represents an escalation in Iran’s campaign to assert control over the waterway, deepening disruption to global oil flows.

item.title: IEA Mulls Emergency Action To Unleash Oil Reserves
item.description: Global energy leaders are gathering to decide whether to release emergency oil reserves as production and export disruptions tied to the Middle East conflict continue to strain global markets. The International Energy Agency's (IEA) Executive Director warned that conditions in oil markets have deteriorated in recent days.

item.title: Tankers Avoid Emirati Port as Gulf War Risk Disrupts Oil Exports
item.description: Shipping companies have grown reluctant to call at the port of Fujairah in the UAE, prompting cancellations of oil cargoes. Adnoc is reselling these cargoes at higher prices, as the port is considered too close for comfort to the conflict zone, even though it is outside the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: US Crude Oil Inventories Sag As Iran War Bolsters Prices
item.description: The American Petroleum Institute (API) estimated that crude oil inventories in the United States fell by 1.7 million barrels in the week ending March 6. This unexpected decline by analysts occurs as the Iran war continues to bolster oil prices.

item.title: Investors Rotate Into Mid-Cap Energy Names as Big Oil Stalls
item.description: Oil prices pulled back sharply for the second day running after U.S. President Trump signaled that the Middle East war is nearing a conclusion. This effectively reduced the 'geopolitical risk premium' that had previously driven prices towards $120 a barrel, leading investors to rotate into mid-cap energy names.

item.title: Middle East crisis could push UK inflation back up to 3%, says OBR
item.description: The UK's Office for Budget Responsibility (OBR) believes inflation could end 2026 at 3% due to the U.S.-Israel war in Iran. This forecast is a percentage point higher than pre-war expectations, primarily driven by the energy price shock triggered by the Middle East crisis.

item.title: The Wisdom of Europe’s “Great Capitulation”
item.description: The European Union was widely criticized for agreeing to an asymmetrical trade deal with the US last summer. However, in doing so, it effectively ensured that the US would continue harming itself by imposing high costs on its own businesses and consumers, while Europe took the economically sound step of lowering its residual tariffs.

item.title: Stock market today: Dow, S&P 500, Nasdaq futures slip amid continued Iran fallout, with CPI on deck
item.description: Futures on major U.S. stock indices, the Dow, S&P 500, and Nasdaq, slipped. This decline is attributed to the continued fallout from the Iran conflict, with investors awaiting upcoming Consumer Price Index (CPI) data.

item.title: Federal Reserve Board announces termination of enforcement actions with Industrial and Commercial Bank of China Ltd., Industrial and Commercial Bank of China Ltd., New York Branch, Standard Chartered PLC, and Standard Chartered Bank
item.description: The Federal Reserve Board announced the termination of enforcement actions with Industrial and Commercial Bank of China Ltd. and Standard Chartered PLC. This decision applies to both the main entities and their New York branches, indicating the resolution of previous regulatory issues.
